note: Biblical majuscule | Similar to Vindob. theol. gr. 31, Paris. suppl. gr. 1286, and Codex Purpureus Rossanensis, suggesting a date in the sixth century (501-600 CE) and a place of origin in the region of Syria or Antioch (cf. G. Cavallo, Ricerche sulla maiuscola biblica (Florence, 1967), pp. 98-104; P. Orsini, Manoscritti in maiuscola biblica. Materiali per un aggiornamento (Cassino, 2005), pp. 176-177, 201-203, 210-211

bib.citation: Bensley, Robert Lubbock, James Rendel Harris, and Francis C. Burkitt. The Four Gospels in Syriac transcribed from the Sinaitic Palimpsest. Cambridge, 1894
Lewis, Agnes Smith. Select Narratives of Holy Women from the Syro-Antiochene or Sinai Palimpsest. Studia Sinaitica IX. London: 1900
Hjelt, Arthur, ed. Syrus sinaiticus. Helsingfors, 1930
Guignard, Christophe. “0323: A Forgotten 4th or 5th Century Greek Fragment of the Gospel of John in the Syrus Sinaiticus.” Novum Testamentum 57 (2015)

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
